title: filter
text: filter Disable or enable debugging in a trigger. filter { Trigger | off } Arguments Trigger —trigger abbreviation; causes all ProcScript statements in the trigger to be executed without pausing for debugging commands. off —remove the filter and restore debugging in Trigger. none—display the trigger currently being filtered. Description While a filter is in effect, the debugger does not step through ProcScript instructions in the trigger or any ProcScript modules called from the trigger. Any breakpoints in the trigger are ignored. Only one filter can be active at a time. By default there is a filter on the preDisplay trigger. This avoids the need to step through these triggers for items on the menu bar each time a component is first displayed. Example: To disable the debugger while the read trigger is active: filter READ Copy Related Links Debugging in Character Mode On a client running in character mode, the debug ProcScript statement causes a Debug command line to be displayed at the bottom of the screen.
